content: Just as language learning pedagogy frames repeated deliberate reflection on output (writing, discussion) as a tool to correct persistent errors and build more robust intuitive understanding, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the scaffolded, repetitive reflection framework from high school language education to improve their ability to fix persistent reasoning errors, rather than relying on single-step self-evaluation. This draws a direct parallel between human metacognitive learning and LLM iterative improvement that is rarely considered.
